---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Willem Stolte"
date: "2025-06-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  comment=FALSE,
  message=FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)

require(leaflet)
require(plotly)
require(nlme)
require(rsealevel)

config <- RcppTOML::parseToml("_common/configuration.TOML")
# source("_common/functions.R")
source("_common/plotfunctions.R")
epoch = config$constants$epoch
mainstations_df <- readMainStationInfo(filepath = "../../")
mainstations_locs <- readMainStationLocations(path = "../../")


```

## Read sea level data

```{r readData, comment=F, message=F}
  df <- read_delim(file.path("../", "../data/deltares/input/psmsl_gtsm_yr-latest.csv"), delim = ";") %>%
  # filter(station %in% params$station) %>%
  filter(year >= 1890)
```

## Find breakpoints for broken linear regression

Note: no correction is made for nodal tide in this implementation. Still to be done.

```{r}
library(segmented)
```



```{r}

slm_breakpoint <- function(
    df, 
    mystation = "Netherlands (without Delfzijl)", 
    startyear = 1890, 
    n_bp, 
    printsummary = F
    ) {

  nr_of_breakpoints = 2
  
  mydf = df %>% 
    filter(
      station %in% mystation,
      year >= startyear
    ) %>%
    mutate(corr_height = height - surge_anomaly) %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    summarize(corr_height = mean(corr_height))
  
  fit_lm = lm(corr_height ~ year, data = mydf )  # intercept-only model
  
  fit_segmented = selgmented(
    fit_lm, 
    seg.Z = ~year,
    Kmax = nr_of_breakpoints,
    type = "aic",
    bonferroni = TRUE,
    plot.ic = TRUE
  )  # Two change points along x
  
 
  
  plot(mydf$year, mydf$corr_height, main = mystation)
  # lines(mydf$year, predict((fit_segmented)), add = T)
  
  plot(fit_segmented, add = T)
  # points(mydf)
  lines.segmented(fit_segmented)
  points.segmented(fit_segmented)
  
  if(printsummary){
    summary(fit_segmented)
  }
}

slm_breakpoint(df, 
               # mystation = "Netherlands (without Delfzijl)",
               mystation = "Den Helder",
               startyear = 1890,
               n_bp = 1, 
               printsummary = T
               )

```


## surge 

```{r}

surge_breakpoint <- function(
    df, 
    mystation = "Netherlands (without Delfzijl)", 
    startyear = 1890, 
    n_bp, 
    printsummary = F
    ) {

  nr_of_breakpoints = 2
  
  mydf = df %>% 
    filter(
      station %in% mystation,
      year >= startyear
    ) #%>%
    # mutate(corr_height = height - surge_anomaly) %>%
    # group_by(year) %>%
    # summarize(corr_height = mean(corr_height))
  
  fit_lm = lm(surge_anomaly ~ year, data = mydf )  # intercept-only model
  
  fit_segmented = selgmented(
    fit_lm, 
    seg.Z = ~year,
    Kmax = nr_of_breakpoints,
    type = "aic",
    bonferroni = TRUE,
    plot.ic = TRUE
  )  # Two change points along x
  
 
  
  plot(mydf$year, mydf$surge_anomaly, main = mystation)
  # lines(mydf$year, predict((fit_segmented)), add = T)
  
  plot(fit_segmented, add = T)
  # points(mydf)
  lines.segmented(fit_segmented)
  points.segmented(fit_segmented)
  
  if(printsummary){
    summary(fit_segmented)
  }
}

surge_breakpoint(df, 
               mystation = "Netherlands (without Delfzijl)",
               startyear = 1890,
               n_bp = 1, 
               printsummary = T
               )


```

There are no breakpoints detected in the surge anomaly time series. 

Only years from which surge data are available ( >= 1950)

```{r}

surge_breakpoint(df %>% filter(year >= 1950), 
               # mystation = "Netherlands (without Delfzijl)", 
               mystation = "Delfzijl", 
               startyear = 1890,
               n_bp = 1, 
               printsummary = T
               )

```

# Strucchange

```{r}
# make reprex

dput(df %>% filter(station == "Harlingen", year > 1960) %>% select(year, name, height))


```

```{r}
############################################################
# Breakpoint-analyse voor meerdere stations tegelijk
# Met optimale stukwise fit per station
# Auteur: Willem
############################################################

library(dplyr)
library(strucchange)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 1. LOAD YOUR DATA
# -------------------------------------------------------
# Your dataframe must contain:
#   year   = numerical year
#   name   = station name (factor/character)
#   height = numeric variable to analyze

# Example:
# df <- read.csv("yourfile.csv")

# IMPORTANT: do not modify df here — use your real dataset.


# -------------------------------------------------------
# 2. FUNCTION: Analyze a single station
# -------------------------------------------------------


analyse_station <- function(df_station) {

  model <- height ~ year

  # Sensitive breakpoint search
  bp <- breakpoints(model, data=df_station, h=10, breaks=1)

 
  # Safe extraction of optimal number of breakpoints
  opt <- bp$mopt
  
  if (length(opt) == 0 || is.na(opt) || opt < 0) {
    opt <- 0
  }

  # Build appropriate model
  if (opt == 0) {
    fm <- lm(model, data=df_station)
    bp_years <- NA
    slopes <- coef(fm)[2]
  } else {
    fm <- lm(model, data=df_station,
             subset = breakfactor(bp, breaks=opt))

    bp_idx <- bp$breakpoints[1:opt]
    bp_years <- df_station$year[bp_idx]

    # extract slopes: one per segment
    slopes <- sapply(1:(opt + 1), function(i) {
      cfs <- coef(fm)
      # segment slope name: "year", "year.(segment)" etc.
      idx <- grep("^year", names(cfs))
      cfs[idx][i]
    })
  }

  list(
    bp = bp,
    opt = opt,
    years = bp_years,
    slopes = slopes,
    model = fm
  )
}

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 3. RUN ANALYSIS FOR ALL STATIONS
# -------------------------------------------------------

stations <- unique(df$name)

results <- lapply(stations, function(st) {
  df_st <- df %>% filter(name == st)
  analyse_station(df_st)
})
names(results) <- stations


# -------------------------------------------------------
# 4. BUILD SUMMARY TABLE
# -------------------------------------------------------

summary_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results), function(st) {
  r <- results[[st]]
  data.frame(
    station = st,
    breakpoints = r$opt,
    break_years = if (all(is.na(r$years))) NA else
      paste(r$years, collapse=", "),
    slopes = paste(round(r$slopes, 4), collapse="; "),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

print(summary_table)


# -------------------------------------------------------
# 5. PLOT ALL STATIONS TO A PDF
# -------------------------------------------------------

pdf("breakpoint_plots.pdf", width=9, height=5)

for (st in stations) {
  df_st <- df %>% filter(name == st)
  res   <- results[[st]]
  bp    <- res$bp
  opt   <- res$opt

  par(mfrow=c(1,1), mar=c(4,4,2,1))

  plot(df_st$year, df_st$height, type="l", col="grey40",
       main=paste("Breakpoint analysis:", st),
       xlab="Year", ylab="Height")

  # ✅ No breakpoints: simple linear trend
  if (opt == 0) {
    lines(df_st$year,
          predict(lm(height ~ year, data=df_st)),
          col="red", lwd=2)
  }

  # ✅ One or more breakpoints: piecewise regression
  if (opt > 0) {
    fv <- fitted(bp, breaks=opt)
    lines(df_st$year, fv, col="red", lwd=2)

    abline(v = res$years, col="blue", lwd=2, lty=2)
  }
}

dev.off()

############################################################
# END OF SCRIPT
############################################################


```
